content.full_text:
November 16, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
The Travelers Business Center
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR FORMAL WRITTEN ADVICE PURSUANT TO 2 CCR 18329/
GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 83114(b)

Dear Commission:

My name is Barbara Williamson. I was elected as a Council Member for the City Council of the City of Simi Valley on November 3, 1992, for a four year term. My mailing address is: City Hall, 2929 Tapo Canyon Road, Simi Valley, CA 93063.

The facts giving rise to my request for advice are as follows:

1. I am employed as a vice president of Simi Valley Bank and intend to continue that employment concurrent with my holding of the office of Council Member for the City of Simi Valley. Simi Valley Bank is a source of income to me according to the provisions of the Political Reform Act. As a vice president of the bank, I am an employee, not a board member or stockholder.

2. The City Council, prior to my election, entered into a contract with Simi Valley Bank for various banking services for the City. The bank derives income under this contract, which includes various checking accounts as well as certificates of deposit. The amount of that income in 1991 was in excess of $20,000.

3. The City Council votes approximately twice per month to authorize "warrants" drawn against checking accounts with Simi Valley Bank. These transactions have an impact which cannot be determined with certainty upon the assets or income of the bank.

4. The Council periodically votes on investment decisions and on investment policy decisions which could directly or indirectly affect Simi Valley Bank. The amount of such effect cannot be calculated or estimated with any degree of certainty.

5. The Council periodically votes on matters affecting individuals or firms which bank with Simi Valley Bank, and the Council's decisions concerning those individuals or firms could have an indirect affect upon the assets or income of the bank in amounts which are incapable of calculation.


I apologize, but this document image is too faint or poorly scanned for me to provide an accurate verbatim transcription. The text is not legible enough to distinguish individual words and sentences with confidence. A higher quality scan or image would be needed to complete this transcription task accurately.

Fair Political Practices Commission
November 16, 1992
Page 2


6.    Simi Valley Bank operates a branch office in a City Redevelopment Study
      Area. I manage this branch office. The Bank occupies the building under
      a lease.

REQUEST FOR ADVICE

In view of the foregoing facts:

a)    May I participate in votes on the authorization of warrants drawn on Simi
      Valley Bank?

b)    Am I prohibited from voting on any request for proposals for bank
      services, or for any contract extension, termination, etc. involving Simi
      Valley Bank or any bank which might seek to compete with Simi Valley Bank
      for the City's banking services contract?

c)    Am I prohibited from participating in deliberation or voting on
      investments or investment policy which may directly or indirectly affect
      Simi Valley Bank?

d)    Am I prohibited from participating in redevelopment decisions concerning
      the area where my Bank branch is located?

e)    With regard to business firms or individuals with matters before the City
      Council, am I obliged to investigate where persons or firms having matters
      before the Council do their banking or whether they otherwise have any
      ties to Simi Valley Bank prior to participating in their matter? If they
      have such a tie to the bank, must I disqualify myself from consideration
      of the matter?

f)    A large number of City employees have checking/savings accounts with Simi
      Valley Bank, balances varying from a few hundred dollars to several
      thousand dollars. Some City employees may have loans with Simi Valley
      Bank. May I legally participate in decisions involving wages, hours, and
      other terms and conditions of employment for such employees?

I realize that some of my questions may be difficult to answer because detailed
facts cannot be given at this time. However, without a general response I will
be severely restricted in my Council duties due to the time constraints between
staff report availability and the time for rendering a Council decision (four
days). I know that you cannot respond in writing to any request for advice in
less than 21 working days, and I am aware that while the City Attorney can give
me some guidance on the Political Reform Act, and telephone advice from the FPPC
staff can be obtained in less than four days, neither will protect me to the
extent that a formal advice letter will in the event that an allegation of a
violation of the Act is brought against me.

Fair Political Practices Commission
November 16, 1992
Page 3


I am also aware that some of the above issues may relate to Government Code
Section 1090 et. seq. which is not within the jurisdiction of the F.P.P.C. I do
not expect your advice letter to cover that area of the law.

Thank you for your assistance. If you need further information, I may be reached
at (805) 581-2800 x300 or in writing at City Hall.

Very,truly yours,

[signature]
Barbara Williamson

BW:ldc


ltr.165
